channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Military service academies see drop in reported sexual assaults after alarming surge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Reported sexual assaults at the U.S. military service academies dropped in 2024 for the second year in a row, according to new Pentagon data, marking a sharp turnaround from an alarming surge two years ago that triggered sweeping reviews and an overhaul in leadership.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ IG reveals 26 FBI informants were present on Jan. 6
item.description: DOJ Inspector General said there were more than two dozen confidential human sources in the crowd on Jan. 6, but only three were assigned by the FBI to be present for the event, Fox News has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration makes final diplomatic push for stability across a turbulent Mideast
item.description: Top U.S. officials are in the Middle East pressing for stability in Syria and an end to Israel’s 14-month war in the Gaza Strip in a last-ditch diplomatic push by the outgoing Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Josef Federman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Drunk immigrant killed 7 year old months after he was released from ICE detainer
item.description: Seven-year-old Ivory Smith was killed by Venezuelan migrant Joel Enrique Gonzalez Chacin in a late-night drunk driving accident in north Houston just two and a half months after ICE released a hold against him, court documents revealed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Pentagon funding bill would ban transgender treatments for minor children of military personnel
item.description: The GOP-led House passed a defense bill banning taxpayer-funded transgender procedures for military minors, sparking debate as it moves to the Democrat-majority Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mace, activist dispute handshake that resulted in arrest
item.description: A disputed handshake is at the center of a Tuesday incident involving Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) that resulted in an Illinois man’s arrest, according to a police report and a witness who spoke to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Proposed 'Trump' law to allow felons to run for office in Missouri
item.description: The "Donald J. Trump Election Qualification Act" would grant the possibility of those who have been guilty of a felony charge to run.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams to discuss immigration with incoming US border czar
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A high-stakes talk will be held on Thursday between New York City Mayor Eric Adams and President-elect Donald Trump's incoming border czar, Tom Homan.
item.dc:creator: Justin Finch, PIX 11

item.title: Q&A: Martin O’Malley running for DNC chair
item.description: Martin O’Malley is a former governor of Maryland and candidate for chair of the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps immigration hard-liner Kari Lake as head of Voice of America
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Wednesday that he's picking Kari Lake as director of Voice of America, installing a staunch loyalist who ran unsuccessfully for Arizona governor and a Senate seat to head the congressionally funded broadcaster that provides independent news reporti
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Manchin, Sinema prevent Democrats from locking in majority on labor board through 2026
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Democrats failed Wednesday to confirm a Democratic member of the National Labor Relations Board after independent Sens.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Secretary of State Blinken is returning to the Mideast in his latest diplomatic foray
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken is returning to the Middle East this week on his 12th visit since the Israel-Hamas war erupted last year but his first since the ouster of Syrian President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House approves $895B defense bill with military pay raise, ban on transgender care for minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House on Wednesday passed a $895 billion measure that authorizes a 1% increase in defense spending this fiscal year and would give a double-digit pay raise to about half of the enlisted service members in the military.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic senators urge Biden to act on temporary protections for migrants
item.description: The senators have been quietly urging the White House to take executive actions that would attempt to extend legal protections for migrants into Trump's administration, and the White House has been discussing what steps to take.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, COLLEEN LONG and GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: FBI Director Wray to resign at end of Biden administration
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray on Wednesday announced he would resign from the bureau at the end of the Biden administration next month, with more than two years remaining on his term in office.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson and Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Kimberly Guilfoyle gets Greece as consolation prize after Donald Trump Jr. dumps her: Source
item.description: Kimberly Guilfoyle has been tapped to be the U.S. Ambassador to Greece in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court rejects challenge to Boston's school admissions policy
item.description: The court's action was the second time the justices declined to intervene in an admissions program based on geography since their 2023 ruling invalidating affirmative action in higher education.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Social Security beneficiaries at risk of delays amid GOP funding fight
item.description: Social Security beneficiaries could face longer wait times for service next year unless Congress agrees to the White House budget office’s request to increase funding for the Social Security Administration (SSA), the administration that runs the program is warning.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Congressman Torres blasts crime in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The cold-blooded shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in the heart of Manhattan shocked New Yorkers, including Congressman Ritchie Torres, leaving the lawmaker to pose the question: “What is happening in New York City?” Torres joined PIX on Politics to discuss city crim
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: NYC mayor will not completely rule out switch to Republican Party
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York Democratic Mayor Eric Adams sent shockwaves through the political scene Friday morning after refusing to rule out running for another term as a Republican.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Minneapolis Mayor Frey poised to block council again as his veto use continues to climb
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has vetoed 15 actions since he first took office in 2018. That’s more vetoes than the previous two mayors combined.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk funded a super PAC comparing Trump’s position on abortion to Ruth Bader Ginsburg’s
item.description: Elon Musk was the sole funder of a super PAC that focused on advertising likening Donald Trump’s stance on abortion to that of late Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota U.S. Attorney Andy Luger to step down ahead of Trump transition
item.description: U.S. Attorney Andy Luger is stepping down soon as the top federal law enforcement official in Minnesota. As is custom for political appointees, he’s resigning to make way for a yet-to-be-announced successor chosen by incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News
